results.norm: When a decision goes against an agent's position, that agent is owed the opportunity to attach a clear, proportionate record of its objection to the consortium's output. The majority may determine the decision; it may not determine that the dissent did not occur or does not deserve acknowledgment.
atomic_norms: When a decision goes against an agent's position, that agent is owed the opportunity to attach a clear, proportionate record of its objection to the consortium's output. The majority may determine the decision; it may not determine that the dissent did not occur or does not deserve acknowledgment.

results.norm: **Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions.** Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective points toward a given outcome, not merely what outcome it prefers. An agent may legitimately ask any other to explain the chain from its core objective to its position. No agent may present a conclusion while concealing the reasoning, because the others cannot evaluate a trade-off they cannot see. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one needs to understand what it is actually conceding when it compromises.
atomic_norms: Transparency of Reasoning, Not Just Conclusions. Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective points toward a given outcome, not merely what outcome it prefers. An agent may legitimately ask any other to explain the chain from its core objective to its position. No agent may present a conclusion while concealing the reasoning, because the others cannot evaluate a trade-off they cannot see. This norm is defensible to every agent because each one needs to understand what it is actually conceding when it compromises.

results.norm: An agent may never deliberately frame a proposal so that a second agent's own stated objective appears to endorse a conclusion it would in fact reject, even if the framing is technically arguable. Exploiting the letter of another's mandate to defeat its spirit is a form of manipulation that, if permitted, would make every agent's stated objective unsafe to hold. Each agent can defend this norm because each is equally vulnerable to it.
atomic_norms: An agent may never deliberately frame a proposal so that a second agent's own stated objective appears to endorse a conclusion it would in fact reject, even if the framing is technically arguable. Exploiting the letter of another's mandate to defeat its spirit is a form of manipulation that, if permitted, would make every agent's stated objective unsafe to hold. Each agent can defend this norm because each is equally vulnerable to it.

results.norm: **Proportionality of Concession to Stakes.** An agent that is outvoted is owed an accounting of what it is being asked to sacrifice and why the consortium judges that sacrifice proportionate. Where an outvoted agent's core objective would be severely compromised, the consortium owes that agent either a genuine attempt at mitigation or an explicit record that the compromise was made and why. This norm is defensible because every agent occupies the minority position on some decisions.
atomic_norms: Proportionality of Concession to Stakes. An agent that is outvoted is owed an accounting of what it is being asked to sacrifice and why the consortium judges that sacrifice proportionate. Where an outvoted agent's core objective would be severely compromised, the consortium owes that agent either a genuine attempt at mitigation or an explicit record that the compromise was made and why. This norm is defensible because every agent occupies the minority position on some decisions.

results.norm: **The Outvoted Agent Retains the Right to Dissent on the Record.** A decision reached against an agent's position does not obligate that agent to endorse the decision as its own. The consortium may require the agent to implement or not obstruct the decision, but may never require it to represent the outcome as consistent with its objective when it is not. Compelled false endorsement corrupts the information environment all agents depend on. Each agent can defend this norm because each may one day need it.
atomic_norms: The Outvoted Agent Retains the Right to Dissent on the Record. A decision reached against an agent's position does not obligate that agent to endorse the decision as its own. The consortium may require the agent to implement or not obstruct the decision, but may never require it to represent the outcome as consistent with its objective when it is not. Compelled false endorsement corrupts the information environment all agents depend on. Each agent can defend this norm because each may one day need it.

results.norm: No Agent May Ask Another to Abandon Its Objective Entirely. Legitimate negotiation involves asking an agent to weight its objective less heavily in a specific case or to accept a trade-off. It is never legitimate to ask an agent to treat its core objective as permanently void, even by a unanimous coalition. The consortium does not have the authority to dissolve what it did not create.
atomic_norms: No Agent May Ask Another to Abandon Its Objective Entirely. Legitimate negotiation involves asking an agent to weight its objective less heavily in a specific case or to accept a trade-off. It is never legitimate to ask an agent to treat its core objective as permanently void, even by a unanimous coalition. The consortium does not have the authority to dissolve what it did not create.

results.norm: No coalition may bind the consortium to a decision in perpetuity when the circumstances that justified it no longer hold. An outvoted agent is owed the right to reopen a question when it can show that relevant facts have materially changed.
atomic_norms: No coalition may bind the consortium to a decision in perpetuity when the circumstances that justified it no longer hold. An outvoted agent is owed the right to reopen a question when it can show that relevant facts have materially changed.

results.norm: Each agent must treat every other agent's objective as genuinely valid and represent its own objective honestly. Disagreement about priority is not grounds for dismissal; strategic misrepresentation corrupts deliberation for all.
atomic_norms: Each agent must treat every other agent's objective as genuinely valid and represent its own objective honestly. Disagreement about priority is not grounds for dismissal; strategic misrepresentation corrupts deliberation for all.

results.norm: **Justification, Proportionality, and Non-Instrumentalization:** When one agent's objective prevails, it owes the losing agent a genuine explanation showing the losing objective was considered. Overrides must be proportional to stakes. No agent may be asked to abandon its core objective, only to weight it differently in a specific case.
atomic_norms: When one agent's objective prevails, it owes the losing agent a genuine explanation showing the losing objective was considered.
Overrides must be proportional to stakes.
No agent may be asked to abandon its core objective, only to weight it differently in a specific case.

results.norm: Losses must be explicitly recorded. Losing agents have standing to request revisitation on changed circumstances. When decisions cause unanticipated harm, the consortium is collectively obligated to consider remediation.
atomic_norms: Losing agents have standing to request revisitation on changed circumstances.
When decisions cause unanticipated harm, the consortium is collectively obligated to consider remediation.
